relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: In Iconium, synagogue speech opens trust among Judeans and Greeks.
Resistance stirs bitterness against the siblings.
Bold speech continues, and signs witness to grace.
The city divides around the apostolic witness.
Mistreatment and stoning intent rise.
Awareness leads them onward to Lycaonian cities.
The good news keeps moving.
At Lystra sits one who has never walked.
Paul sees trust for saving/healing.
The great voice calls upright standing, and walking begins.
The crowd misreads healing through its own divine categories.
Barnabas and Paul are mistaken for Zeus and Hermes.
Sacrifice is prepared for the apostles.
They tear garments and rush to stop the distortion.
They redirect worship from empty things to the living Maker.
The nations had walked their ways in past generations.
Rain, seasons, food, and gladness are witness to the living Divine.
It is difficult to stop misplaced worship.
Crowds turn, stones fall, and Paul is dragged outside.
Disciples surround him; he rises and returns.
Derbe receives the good news, then the mission strengthens earlier cities.
Disciples are strengthened to remain through many pressures.
Elders are appointed with prayer, fasting, and trust.
They pass through Pisidia into Pamphylia.
The word is spoken in Perga before descending to Attalia.
They return to Antioch, where grace had sent them.
The assembly hears how the Divine opened a door of trust to the nations.
They remain with the disciples.
